concerns.text: Task T030 mandates 'Leave-One-System-Out (LOSO) CV' if unique groups < 30. The spec (Edge Cases) explicitly defines the fallback behavior for insufficient groups (< 10) as 'log a warning... falling back to standard bootstrap'. Introducing LOSO CV is a semantic weakening of the spec's defined statistical protocol and an unauthorized change to the experimental design.

Task T037 requires calculating 'False Positive Rate (FPR)' for the sensitivity sweep. The spec (FR-006, US-3) explicitly mandates reporting the 'Type I error rate (estimated via permutation testing)'. FPR and Type I error rate are distinct statistical concepts; the task fails to implement the specific verification method required by the spec.

Task T019 defines the target calculation (Residual Bulk Modulus) but omits the mandatory constraint from FR-002 and FR-008 to 'exclude Miedema-derived features from the predictor set'. Without this explicit exclusion step in the task, the implementation risks circular validation, violating the spec's core validity constraint.

Task T005 creates `source_metadata.yaml` but only mentions recording 'query parameters'. FR-010 and Constitution Principle VI explicitly require recording 'API versions' as well. Omitting the API version violates the provenance requirement needed to reproduce the exact data retrieval.

Task T033 mentions checking for residual correlation but fails to specify the mandatory behavior from FR-009: 'log a warning and proceed with caution' if |r| > 0.1. The task leaves the failure mode ambiguous, potentially allowing the pipeline to halt or proceed incorrectly, violating the spec's defined edge case handling.
